title: Pembrolizumab after platinum-based chemotherapy for metastatic urothelial cancer: comparison between patients from a Dutch nationwide cohort and KEYNOTE-045.
abstract: Pending adoption of enfortumab-vedotin plus pembrolizumab in first-line setting, platinum-based chemotherapy remains the standard of care for patients with metastatic or locally advanced urothelial cancer (mUC). Second-line pembrolizumab may be offered to patients with disease progression, based on the KEYNOTE-045 trial. It is unclear how well the findings from KEYNOTE-045 translate to the Dutch mUC patient population in routine clinical practice. The objective of this study was to compare characteristics and outcomes of patients in a nationwide population-based pembrolizumab-treated cohort with the KEYNOTE-045 trial.
